$comment: Externalized harness policy data (HARNESS-020). The scan ENGINE lives in scripts/harness/*; this file holds the project-specific POLICY so the engine is repo-agnostic. Edit values here instead of hardcoding literals in scan scripts.

architectureDocs.$comment: Canonical architecture documents whose prose/diagrams must reference only real workspace packages (check-dependency-direction Rule 9, absorbed from check-architecture-conformance.mjs). packages/*/docs/SPEC.md is always included by the engine.

purity.$comment: Package-purity POLICY (check-dependency-direction Rule 10, absorbed from check-sdk-react-free.mjs). Each entry forbids a package from importing OR declaring the listed modules; a missing scan target is a hard finding, not a silent pass (HARNESS-016 / ARL-16g dead-guard guard).
purity.reason: agent-framework is a platform-neutral assembly layer; React hooks/context/components belong in the product/UI packages (agent-cli, agent-transport-tui, agent-transport-gui) only.

consistency.$comment: Policy DATA for scan-consistency.mjs (HARNESS-DIET-003 split). The engine (five exported check functions) lives in scripts/harness/scan-consistency.mjs; the Robota-specific required-script names, guidance scan targets, and phrase blocklists live here. `pattern` fields are RegExp sources (compiled with the optional `flags`). Values moved VERBATIM from the former in-script constants.

skillPhraseChecks.message: Undefined rule-level terminology "Path-Only" should not appear.

skillPhraseChecks.pattern: Lower layers import from higher layers, not the other way around\.

skillPhraseChecks.message: Default state machine examples must not model implicit failed -> retry transitions.

skillPhraseChecks.pattern: do not use `any`, `unknown`, or `\{\}` in production code\.
skillPhraseChecks.message: Legacy type rule contradicts current trust-boundary policy for unknown.
Boundary examples must not teach unchecked casts into owned payload types.

guidancePhraseChecks.message: Use "user execution test scenario" for user-runnable product verification.
Use canonical user execution test scenario terminology instead of legacy shorthand.
Use "user execution test scenario"; do not omit "test" from the term.

neutrality.$comment: Per-subsystem library-neutrality policy DATA (HARNESS-DIET-002). The neutrality scan ENGINES live in scripts/harness/scan-*-neutrality.mjs; their Robota-specific target paths / allowlists / forbidden terms live here so the engines stay repo-agnostic.
